Replacing a specific cryptographic fact with a vague trust word during 'simplification'. 'Verified offline against the embedded public key' must not collapse to 'secure and trusted'. Concision removes filler, never the load-bearing noun.

Adding a marketing eyebrow kicker above a headline to 'frame' it. The card category tag (.card__k, 1 to 3 words) is a label and is fine; an ALLCAPS marketing eyebrow over an H1/H2 is banned. Section heads are a bare H2 plus one lede line.

Introducing a second contact or a soft CTA. Any 'Contact us', 'Get in touch', 'Learn more', or sales@/hello@ address violates the rules. Contact is dev@kolm.ai only; CTAs are 'Start an audit' and 'Verify a report'.

Quantifying with an unmeasured superlative. 'Fastest', 'best-in-class', 'world-class' are both hype AND an unbacked claim. If there is no measured number, use the design-property framing the site already uses ('the target review window'), not a superlative.
directives: BODY MEASURE: set all readable text columns in ch, not px (exact in mono). Body p { max-width: 60ch }. Lede { max-width: 46ch }. H1 { max-width: 16ch }. Card p { max-width: 42ch }. This caps line length at the readability sweet spot for monospace.
HERO H1: <= 8 words, <= 2 lines. CSS: font-weight:600; font-size:clamp(34px,5vw,60px); line-height:1.04; letter-spacing:-0.02em (slight negative tracking tightens wide mono glyphs at display size).
LEDE: <= 2 sentences and <= 28 words total. CSS: font-size:18px; line-height:1.5; font-weight:400; max-width:46ch; margin-top:14px.

SENTENCE LENGTH: average 12 to 16 words; hard cap 24 words. Max one subordinate clause ('and'/'which'/'that') per sentence; beyond that, split on a period. CI: flag any sentence > 24 words via a build-time check.
PARAGRAPH CAP: <= 3 sentences and <= 45 words anywhere in body. CARD BODY: 1 to 2 sentences, <= 24 words, <= 3 lines at the card measure.

LEAD TOKEN: the first 1 to 3 words of every line carry the noun or number. Hoist figures to the front: '10x', '0%', '4-8 weeks', 'Ed25519', 'Days'. In mono this aligns the left edge into a scan index.

EMPHASIS BY WEIGHT: 400 body / 500 inline key noun or fact / 600 headlines and crypto facts. Green #11875A restricted to .btn--primary and .badge--ok / pass-state only. Never font-weight-bold a green word in running text.
TOKEN NO-WRAP: every hash, key fingerprint, signature and count gets white-space:nowrap; font-weight:500 so 'ed25519:7HHxmVcZ...', '410302c9...', '0 servers', '6 findings' never break mid-token.

LIST TRIGGER: any sentence with 3+ comma-joined coordinate items or a parenthetical enumeration becomes <ul class="ledger"> { list-style:none; display:grid; gap:8px } with a 1ch green tick marker; each row a noun phrase <= 6 words.

DELETE-ON-SIGHT BANLIST: very, really, simply, just, actually, basically, essentially, leverage, robust, seamless, powerful, cutting-edge, world-class, best-in-class, enterprise-grade, next-generation, revolutionary, frictionless, delightful, holistic. Rewrites: 'in order to'->'to'; 'is able to'/'has the ability to'->'can'; 'allows you to'/'enables you to'/'helps you'->use the verb directly; drop 'we believe'/'we're excited'/'designed to'/'aims to'.

PROTECTED STRINGS (pass verbatim, never soften): 'tested and reported, not warranted'; 'assessed'; 'is not proof'; 'target'; 'design property'; 'covers the canonical bytes'. CI grep must confirm 'tested and reported, not warranted' still present after any edit.

PUNCTUATION: no em (U+2014) or en (U+2013) dash anywhere. Split clauses with a period or semicolon. Hyphen only inside compound modifiers ('four-to-eight-week', 'append-only'). Add a CI grep that fails on U+2014, U+2013, and the substring 'honest'.

CONTACT + CTA LOCK: contact is dev@kolm.ai only (no sales@/hello@/support@). Allowed CTA verbs: 'Start an audit', 'Verify a report', 'See a signed report', 'Download the JSON'. Banned: 'Contact us', 'Get in touch', 'Learn more', 'Request a demo'.
NO EYEBROW KICKER: section heads are a bare H2 plus one lede line, no ALLCAPS marketing label above them. The .card__k category tag (1 to 3 words) is permitted as a label only.

HEADLINE FORM: pick one per heading, max 8 words: (A) correction couplet 'X isn't Y. It's Z.'; (B) imperative pair/triad 'Verb. Verb.'; (C) subject-collapse 'The N is the proof.'; (D) number-lead 'Six controls, mapped to the frameworks reviewers cite.'
ALLCAPS RUN CAP: readable copy ALLCAPS tokens <= 24 chars (VOID, VERIFIED, ED25519, PASS). Longer = sentence case. Decorative microprint is the sole exception and stays aria-hidden.
READABILITY GATE: target Flesch-Kincaid grade 7 to 9, average sentence < 16 words. Run Hemingway/FK on hero + each section head + each lede before ship; any 'hard to read' sentence is split.

BEFORE/AFTER 1 (hero lede, 42w -> 25w, 2 sentences): BEFORE 'Your biggest enterprise contract stalls the moment a CISO has to vet an autonomous agent. kolm audits the agent and issues a cryptographically signed evidence report your buyer verifies offline, against your public key. A four-to-eight-week review goes back to days.' AFTER 'One CISO review can stall your biggest deal for two months. kolm signs evidence your buyer verifies offline against your key, so weeks become days.'

BEFORE/AFTER 2 (problem lede, 39w -> 26w): BEFORE 'Nobody turns an autonomous agent loose on their customers' data because the vendor says it's safe. The review that used to take a week now runs four to eight, and a filled-in questionnaire no longer clears the bar.' AFTER 'No CISO trusts an autonomous agent on customer data on your say-so. The week-long review now runs four to eight, and a questionnaire no longer clears it.'

BEFORE/AFTER 3 (card body, 35w -> 19w, number-lead): BEFORE 'Most agents carry roughly ten times the privileges they actually use, and many share one API key across tools. It is the first thing a reviewer flags, and the hardest to wave away.' AFTER '10x the privileges they actually use, often on one shared key. It is the first thing a reviewer flags.'

BEFORE/AFTER 4 (vault lede, 62w -> 33w, fragment for rhythm): BEFORE 'To the right is a real, signed audit of a deliberately over-permissioned demo agent, which is why it scores 0%. The check runs entirely in this browser. Press Inflate the score to raise the readiness number, the way a vendor under pressure might. The seal fractures and the report reads VOID, every time. That is the whole point.' AFTER 'This is a real signed audit of an over-permissioned demo agent. It scores 0%. The check runs in your browser. Press Inflate the score. The seal fractures and the report reads VOID. Every time.'

BEFORE/AFTER 5 (ai-vendors lede, one 39-word sentence -> lede + ledger): BEFORE 'Instead of promising, you arrive with a signed report mapped to the exact frameworks the buyer's group cites (permissions, audit trail, data egress, injection), each finding in their vocabulary, each fact checkable offline against your public key.' AFTER lede: 'You arrive with a signed report in the buyer's own framework language.' + ledger rows: 'Mapped to the controls their group cites' / 'Each finding in their vocabulary' / 'Each fact checkable offline against your key'.

BEFORE/AFTER 6 (how-it-works verify lede, 48w -> 26w + micro-line): BEFORE 'Your buyer opens the report and the signature is checked in their browser, against the public key inside it. Because kolm is never in the path, there is no back end that could fake a green check. Pin the issuer key to also prove it's the key you expected.' AFTER 'Your buyer checks the signature in their browser, against the key inside the report. kolm is never in the path, so no server can fake a pass.' + micro-line: 'Pin the issuer key to confirm it is yours.'

topic: Premium light-paper backdrop + rationed signal-green accent system for kolm's mono-forward "signed instrument / cryptographic ledger" audit site (#F7F8F6 paper, #0F1411 ink, #11875A green)
keyFindings: GREEN IS NOT A TEXT COLOR. #11875A on #F7F8F6 measures 4.25:1 — below the 4.5:1 AA threshold for body text and links, but above the 3:1 threshold for large text, fills, seals, borders and focus rings. Verified by computation. This single fact dictates that green may only ever appear as a FILL or a LARGE/UI element, never as running type.

White-on-green (#FFFFFF on #11875A) is 4.53:1 — it clears AA normal-text by 0.03, so primary-button labels are legal but only at >=16px/600 weight. Do not set button text smaller or lighter, and deepen to #0F7A50 (5.36:1) on hover so the press state is comfortably above threshold.

The backdrop should be exactly TWO layers and never more: ONE geometric texture (a masked dot-matrix that reads as ledger/graph paper) plus ONE colorless tonal wash. Stacking two textures (e.g. grain + grid) is the single fastest way to look amateur on near-white.

For a 'ledger' brand the instinct is ruled horizontal lines — resist it. Repeating full-width horizontal rules make an all-mono page read as a code listing, the exact failure the founder called out. A dot-matrix carries the same ledger/instrument semantics while staying quiet.

The premium signal is the MASK, not the texture. SOTA light sites (Linear, Vercel) concentrate the grid behind the hero/fold and fade it to pure paper within ~600px. 'Wallpaper to all four edges' is what cheap sites do; a fade is what expensive sites do.

Backdrop must cost zero scroll repaint and zero network: render it as one fixed/absolute pseudo-element (z-index:-1, pointer-events:none) using a CSS radial-gradient for the dots — no image request, painted once, GPU-cheap.

Green only reads as 'PASS/VERIFIED' if it is scarce. Budget: at most ONE green fill and ONE green seal per viewport. Two competing greens on screen destroys the semantic — it stops meaning 'verified' and becomes mere decoration.
Disabled states must DRAIN the green to neutral (#E6EBE7 / #9AA39E). A greyed-but-still-green button reads as 'verified-but-dead,' which is semantically incoherent for an evidence/audit product.

A single focus token works site-wide: #11875A passes 3:1 against paper (4.25) AND against the soft green pill fill (3.96). One outline rule covers every interactive element — no per-context overrides needed.

Hairline #E2E5E0 is 1.19:1 vs paper — perfect for decorative ledger rules and section seams, but invisible as a functional control border. Anything that must be perceived as a boundary (input borders, the report card edge) needs ink-at-alpha, not the hairline.

Mono feels premium through restraint of the type grid, so the backdrop must never fight it: dot pitch (24px) should be visually unrelated to the baseline grid, and texture opacity capped at 0.05 so glyph edges always win the contrast battle.

State must not be encoded in green alone (WCAG 1.4.1). Every 'verified/pass' element pairs fill + a checkmark/seal glyph + a text label, so colorblind reviewers and the buyer's security team read PASS without relying on hue.

patterns.name: Hero-scoped masked dot-matrix (ledger grain)
patterns.whatItIs: An ultra-faint dot grid evoking graph/ledger paper, rendered as a single CSS radial-gradient and softly masked so it lives only behind the hero and fades to pure paper before the first content section.
patterns.howToApply: On the hero section only: .hero{position:relative} .hero::before{content:'';position:absolute;inset:0;z-index:-1;pointer-events:none;background-image:radial-gradient(circle at 1px 1px, rgba(15,20,17,0.045) 1px, transparent 1.6px);background-size:24px 24px;-webkit-mask-image:linear-gradient(180deg,#000 0%,rgba(0,0,0,0.5) 45%,transparent 80%);mask-image:linear-gradient(180deg,#000 0%,rgba(0,0,0,0.5) 45%,transparent 80%)}. Cap dot alpha at 0.05. Reuse the identical layer on the final CTA band so the page opens and closes on the same ledger texture and the middle stays clean paper.

patterns.name: Colorless tonal lift wash
patterns.whatItIs: A barely-there radial of near-white over paper that lifts the top of the page so it never looks like a flat fill — depth without any second color, preserving green's scarcity.
patterns.howToApply: On body, global: body{background:radial-gradient(110% 70% at 50% -8%, #FFFFFF 0%, rgba(255,255,255,0) 55%), #F7F8F6}. This is white-on-paper only — do NOT tint it green; a green wash would dilute the pass/verified semantic. It is the one treatment that is allowed everywhere.

patterns.name: Instrument frame (ledger margin rules)
patterns.whatItIs: Two full-height 1px hairlines bounding the content column, echoing the ruled margin of a printed financial instrument or signed certificate — structural, not decorative.
patterns.howToApply: Wrap content in a centered column max-width 76ch (~840px) with border-left/right:1px solid #E2E5E0 running the full document height. Pair with a 1px #E2E5E0 top border on each <section> as a horizontal 'rule line.' Drop the side rules below 640px. This is what makes the page read as a signed instrument rather than a web page.

patterns.name: Green rationing ladder
patterns.whatItIs: A fixed semantic spend of the single accent: primary CTA fill, the verified seal/state, the focus ring, and at most one hairline accent — and nothing else.
patterns.howToApply: Allowed green surfaces, in priority order: (1) primary button fill #11875A, (2) verified seal + soft pill #E8F2EC, (3) focus ring #11875A, (4) exactly ONE 2px green left-border on the live evidence/report card. Forbidden: green body text, green links, green icons in nav, green section headers, green dividers. Audit every page against this 4-item list.

patterns.name: Verified seal token
patterns.whatItIs: The hero asset of the brand: a compact 'VERIFIED / Ed25519' chip that is the ONLY place green and ink combine into a badge, mirroring the signed-report payoff.
patterns.howToApply: Pill: background #E8F2EC, border 1px #CFE3D8, radius 6px, padding 4px 10px; a checkmark or seal glyph in #0C6B45 + label in #0F1411 set in Spline Sans Mono 12px/600 with +0.04em tracking, uppercase. Never set the seal label in green (it would be <4.5:1); keep label ink, glyph green.

patterns.name: Single focus-ring token
patterns.whatItIs: One outline definition that satisfies 3:1 against every background it can land on, removing per-component focus styling.
patterns.howToApply: :focus-visible{outline:2px solid #11875A;outline-offset:2px;border-radius:inherit}. Verified 4.25:1 on paper and 3.96:1 on the green pill — both >=3:1. Remove default browser outlines only with :focus-visible (never :focus) so keyboard users keep it.

patterns.name: SVG grain (alternative texture, not additive)
patterns.whatItIs: An organic fractal-noise grain as a SUBSTITUTE for the dot-matrix when a softer, paper-fibre feel is wanted — chosen instead of, never alongside, the dots.
patterns.howToApply: Inline data-URI: feTurbulence type='fractalNoise' baseFrequency='0.9' numOctaves='2', layer opacity 0.022, on the same fixed z-index:-1 pseudo-element, masked identically to the hero. ~1.2KB, cacheable. Decision rule: ship the dot-matrix (geometric, on-brand for a cryptographic ledger); reserve grain only if the founder finds dots too 'techy.' Never run both.

references.name: Linear — masked dot/grid on near-white
references.whyItMatters: The canonical reference for a faint top-weighted matrix that fades out; demonstrates the 'mask, don't tile' principle and the exact restraint level (~0.04 alpha dots) this brief should target.

references.name: Vercel / Geist design system
references.whyItMatters: Proves a near-white, mono-adjacent, hairline-framed aesthetic can feel premium with almost no color; their 1px border discipline is the template for the instrument-frame pattern.

references.name: Stripe — radial wash + color restraint
references.whyItMatters: Benchmark for using a single soft tonal wash for depth and rationing one accent so it reads as meaningful; validates spending the brand color only on primary action, never on chrome.

references.name: Radix Colors / Tailwind green scale methodology
references.whyItMatters: Shows how to derive a perceptually-even hover/press/fill/border/text ramp from one hue with guaranteed contrast steps — the method behind the #11875A -> #0F7A50 -> #0C6B45 -> #E8F2EC ladder.

references.name: WCAG 2.2 SC 1.4.3 (text) + 1.4.11 (non-text) + 1.4.1 (use of color)
references.whyItMatters: The compliance floor for an elite-security-researcher audience: 1.4.3 forbids green body text at 4.25:1, 1.4.11 permits green fills/rings at 3:1, 1.4.1 requires the seal to carry an icon+label not hue alone.

references.name: Resend / Mintlify docs aesthetic
references.whyItMatters: Real-world proof that a mono-forward, light, hairline-ruled layout reads as 'precision instrument' rather than 'code listing' when texture is subtractive and type measure is disciplined.

pitfalls: Setting any green text or green links on paper — at 4.25:1 it fails AA and looks washed; the whole site's credibility with security reviewers dies on a contrast checker. Green is fill-only.
Tiling the dot-matrix across the entire page with no mask. Full-bleed texture on near-white looks like a default Bootstrap background; the fade is the entire point.
Using repeating horizontal ruled lines to evoke a 'ledger' — it instantly reads as a monospace code listing, the precise drift the founder rejected.

Animating the backdrop (parallax dots, drifting gradient, shimmer). On a 'signed instrument' brand any motion in the paper undermines the stillness/permanence the product sells, and it forces a prefers-reduced-motion branch for zero upside.
Letting two green elements share a viewport (e.g. green CTA + green nav highlight + green icon). Scarcity is what makes green mean PASS; abundance makes it mean nothing.
Tinting the tonal wash green for 'brand cohesion.' A green haze spends the accent on nothing and weakens the seal — keep the wash colorless (#FFFFFF over #F7F8F6).
Greying out disabled buttons while keeping them green-ish. A dead-but-verified-colored control is semantically broken for an evidence product; disabled must go fully neutral.

Relying on the #E2E5E0 hairline (1.19:1) for functional borders like form inputs or the report-card edge — it's invisible as a control boundary; use rgba(15,20,17,0.14) or darker for anything that must read as an edge.
Pushing dot opacity above ~0.06 or pitch below 20px to 'make it visible' — it then competes with mono glyph edges and the page reads busy/noisy instead of crisp.

Encoding the verified state in color alone. Without a checkmark glyph + 'VERIFIED' label, colorblind reviewers (and a skeptical security team) can't read the single most important state in the product.
directives: Token set (CSS custom properties): --paper:#F7F8F6; --ink:#0F1411; --ink-muted:#5B6661 (5.6:1, secondary text/captions); --hairline:#E2E5E0; --green:#11875A; --green-hover:#0F7A50; --green-press:#0C6B45; --green-text:#0D6E48; --green-fill:#E8F2EC; --green-border:#CFE3D8; --disabled-bg:#E6EBE7; --disabled-text:#9AA39E.

Backdrop is exactly two layers: (A) global colorless wash on body — background:radial-gradient(110% 70% at 50% -8%, #FFFFFF 0%, rgba(255,255,255,0) 55%), #F7F8F6; (B) hero-scoped masked dot-matrix only. Nothing else.

Dot-matrix CSS (hero ::before, position:absolute; inset:0; z-index:-1; pointer-events:none): background-image:radial-gradient(circle at 1px 1px, rgba(15,20,17,0.045) 1px, transparent 1.6px); background-size:24px 24px.

Mask the dot-matrix so it dies before content: -webkit-mask-image:linear-gradient(180deg,#000 0%,rgba(0,0,0,0.5) 45%,transparent 80%); mask-image: same. Repeat the identical layer on the final CTA band; leave all mid-page sections as pure paper + wash.
Hi-DPI: in @media (min-resolution:2dppx) raise dot radius to 1.25px and keep alpha <=0.05. Never exceed 0.06 alpha or go below 20px pitch.

prefers-reduced-motion: backdrop is fully static by default. Permit at most ONE 320ms ease-out opacity fade-in of the hero ::before, gated inside @media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference); under reduce, render the final opacity immediately. No parallax, no animated gradients, ever.

Instrument frame: center content in a column max-width:76ch (~840px) with border-left:1px solid #E2E5E0 and border-right:1px solid #E2E5E0 full document height; each <section> gets border-top:1px solid #E2E5E0. Remove the side rules under 640px.

Primary button: background:#11875A; color:#FFFFFF; font:600 16px 'Spline Sans Mono'; letter-spacing:0.01em; padding:12px 20px; radius:8px. Hover background:#0F7A50; active/pressed background:#0C6B45. (White-on-green = 4.53:1 AA at base, 5.36:1 hover, 6.55:1 pressed.)
Button focus and all interactive focus: outline:2px solid #11875A; outline-offset:2px; via :focus-visible only. One rule site-wide (4.25:1 on paper, 3.96:1 on pill — both >=3:1).
Disabled primary button: background:#E6EBE7; color:#9AA39E; cursor:not-allowed; no green, no shadow.
Secondary/ghost button: background:transparent; color:#0F1411; border:1px solid rgba(15,20,17,0.16); hover background:rgba(15,20,17,0.04). No green on secondary actions.
Links: color:#0F1411; text-decoration:underline; text-decoration-color:rgba(15,20,17,0.30); text-underline-offset:3px; hover decoration-color:#0F1411. Links are NEVER green.
Any place a green word is unavoidable (e.g. inline 'Verified'), use --green-text #0D6E48 (5.9:1 on paper, 5.49:1 on pill) — never #11875A, which fails as text.

Verified seal/pill: background:#E8F2EC; border:1px solid #CFE3D8; radius:6px; padding:4px 10px; checkmark glyph in #0C6B45; label in #0F1411, Spline Sans Mono 12px/600, letter-spacing:0.04em, text-transform:uppercase. Must include both glyph AND label (do not encode state in color alone).
The single permitted green hairline accent: a 2px solid #11875A left border on the live evidence/report card only. No other green borders anywhere.
Green budget enforcement: max ONE green fill + ONE green seal per viewport. Build a checklist gate and fail any page that shows two competing greens above the fold.
Functional borders (inputs, the report card, data rows) use border:1px solid rgba(15,20,17,0.14) — NOT the 1.19:1 #E2E5E0 hairline, which is reserved for decorative ledger rules and section seams.
Body text: #0F1411 on #F7F8F6 (17.5:1); secondary/meta text: #5B6661 (5.6:1). Keep body measure at 66–72ch and line-height 1.6 so the mono column never reads as code.

Implement the whole backdrop as pseudo-elements with pointer-events:none and z-index:-1; do not add real DOM nodes, do not use background-attachment:fixed (mobile repaint cost), and request zero image assets for the dot grid.

If grain is chosen instead of dots: inline SVG data-URI, feTurbulence baseFrequency=0.9 numOctaves=2, layer opacity 0.022, same fixed pseudo-element + same mask. It REPLACES the dot-matrix; the two textures are mutually exclusive.

Section rhythm to keep mono premium: section vertical padding 96px desktop / 56px mobile; max ONE accent element (button or seal) per section; headings in Spline Sans Mono 600 with -0.01em tracking to tighten the mono cadence.

Pre-launch QA gates: (1) automated contrast scan must show zero green text under 4.5:1; (2) every 'pass/verified' element must contain a non-color cue; (3) grep the codebase for green hex used as a 'color' on text/links and remove; (4) confirm no animation on the backdrop layers.

topic: Accessibility + Information Architecture spec for kolm.ai "Verified" light theme (mono-forward, 28-page agent-security-evidence site, deal-unblocker positioning)
keyFindings: Measured against paper #F7F8F6 (relative luminance 0.9354), a clean 4-step ink ramp covers every use: #0F1411 = 17.5:1 (AAA, body+headings), #3A413D = 9.8:1 (AAA, secondary), #565B57 = 6.5:1 (AA-safe down to 12px meta), #8B918C = 3.0:1 (NON-text only: disabled, decorative, large numerals, min interactive border). Only the last fails text contrast and must never carry copy.

The brand green #11875A is 4.25:1 on paper, so it FAILS AA (4.5:1) for normal-size text by a hair. Split green into two tokens: --accent #11875A for fills/icons/large(>=24px) text + UI borders, and --accent-text #0B6B45 (6.15:1) for ANY green text <=18px (inline links, 'Verified' labels, small badges). This keeps green scarce and on-palette while staying legible.

White (#FFFFFF) on the #11875A button = 4.53:1: passes AA but with thin margin, so lock the label to weight 500 at >=15px and never use 400/light or a tint. Disabled button = #8B918C text on #EDEFEC, no green.

Hairline #E2E5E0 is only 1.19:1 against paper: correct for decorative dividers and card outlines, but it CANNOT be the sole border of an interactive control. Form-field, toggle and segmented-control borders must step up to #8B918C (3.0:1) to satisfy WCAG 1.4.11 Non-text Contrast.

Mono at display size reads like a code listing unless it is typeset: apply negative tracking (-0.02em on headings, -0.01em on body), drive hierarchy with SIZE + WHITESPACE + one weight step (not color), cap measure at ~66ch, and restrict to weights 400/500/600. This is how an all-Spline-Sans-Mono site reads premium instead of terminal.

Reveal/scroll animations must DEFAULT to visible (opacity:1) and only animate when motion + JS are present. The prior build shipped sections stuck at opacity:0 when the reveal class was not applied (the W921 'bleed'); progressive enhancement plus a hard prefers-reduced-motion reset prevents content from vanishing.

Conversion spine for a deal-unblocker (not a compliance vendor): problem -> how-it-works -> the signed report -> verify-it-yourself -> founder/buyer split -> trust -> single CTA. One primary green CTA per viewport, flagship capped at ~9 sections; everything else (research, changelog, status, enterprise) lives in footer + contextual links.

44x44px target floor (AAA 2.5.5) is the right bar for a security-researcher audience and trivial in mono: nav rows reach 44px via vertical padding even when the label is 14px, and adjacent targets get an 8px minimum gap.

Backdrop is FLAT #F7F8F6: premium comes from a strict vertical rhythm (80px desktop / 40px mobile between sections) and 1px #E2E5E0 hairlines, not gradients or foil. Cards = #FFFFFF fill + #E2E5E0 1px border + barely-there shadow (0 1px 2px rgba(15,20,17,.04)).

Legal pages read thin AND wordy at the same time when dumped as a wall of clauses. A single shared shell (last-updated stamp + plain-language TL;DR box + sticky ToC + collapsible sections) makes /privacy /terms /dpa /baa /sla scannable and credible to a reviewer.

Heading order is structural, not stylistic: exactly one h1 (the page promise), no skipped levels, size controlled by class. Unlabeled regions (Proof bar, FAQ) get a visually-hidden h2 so the screen-reader outline stays complete.
Mobile reflow at 390px is the make-or-break: 16px minimum body (prevents iOS auto-zoom), single column <=640px, and data tables convert to stacked key:value cards rather than silently overflowing.

patterns.name: Two-tier signal green
patterns.whatItIs: Green carries two jobs (primary CTA + verified/pass semantics) at two contrast levels, so one token cannot serve both legibly.
patterns.howToApply: Define --accent:#11875A (fills, icons, >=24px text, UI borders; 4.25:1) and --accent-text:#0B6B45 (any green text <=18px; 6.15:1). Pass-state pills: #0B6B45 glyph + label on a #E9F3EE tint. CTA: #FFFFFF/500 on #11875A. Never recolor body links green at body size with #11875A.

patterns.name: Ink ramp by role, not by shade
patterns.whatItIs: A 4-step grey scale where each step is pinned to a contrast budget and an allowed use, removing guesswork.
patterns.howToApply: --ink:#0F1411 (17.5:1) headings+body; --ink-2:#3A413D (9.8:1) secondary/sublabels/captions used as paragraphs; --ink-3:#565B57 (6.5:1) meta/timestamps/eyebrow-labels >=12px; --ink-faint:#8B918C (3.0:1) disabled text, >=24px decorative numerals, AND the minimum border of any interactive control.

patterns.name: Anatomy block (the report as hero artifact)
patterns.whatItIs: A reusable annotated-artifact module that shows the signed evidence report with leader-line callouts to header, checks table, verdicts, and Ed25519 signature block.
patterns.howToApply: Use on /, /report, /verify and /how-it-works. Monospace artifact in a #FFFFFF card, hairline-divided rows, tabular-nums for IDs/hashes, a green #0B6B45 'Verified' stamp, and 3-5 numbered callouts in --ink-3. This is the single most persuasive object on the site; do not bury it below the fold.

patterns.name: Table-to-card reflow
patterns.whatItIs: Responsive transform that converts wide data tables (checks, subprocessors, SLA credits, status components) into readable stacks on narrow screens.
patterns.howToApply: <=640px: each <tr> becomes a card; cell label (from <th>) in --ink-3 11px uppercase +0.06em, value in --ink. If the table is genuinely 2D (status grid), instead wrap in role='region' aria-label tabindex=0 with overflow-x:auto, sticky first column, and a right-edge fade. Never set table font <13px.

patterns.name: Section rhythm + min-content rule
patterns.whatItIs: A fixed vertical cadence plus a floor on what a section may contain, so nothing reads thin and nothing reads padded.
patterns.howToApply: Vertical gap between sections: --s8 (80px) desktop / --s6 (40px) <=640px. Minimum viable section = a heading + 2-4 sentences OR a 3-tile row. If a planned section cannot meet that floor, merge it upward. Max ~9 sections on the flagship, 5-8 on sub-pages.

patterns.name: Legal/reference shell
patterns.whatItIs: One template that makes clause-heavy pages scannable and trustworthy rather than a wall.
patterns.howToApply: Header: H1 + 'Last updated YYYY-MM-DD' (--ink-3) + plain-language TL;DR box (#FFFFFF card, 3-5 bullets). Body: sticky left ToC (>=961px) over collapsible <section> blocks, each h2 + short prose. Footer of page: contact + related legal links. Reuse verbatim across /privacy /terms /dpa /baa /sla /acceptable-use /subprocessors /transparency-log.

references.name: WCAG 2.2 SC 1.4.3 Contrast (Minimum) + 1.4.6 (Enhanced)
references.whyItMatters: Defines the 4.5:1 normal / 3:1 large thresholds every ink and green token here is measured against; the #11875A 4.25:1 text fail and the #0B6B45 fix come straight from this.

references.name: WCAG 2.2 SC 1.4.11 Non-text Contrast
references.whyItMatters: Requires 3:1 for control boundaries and UI components, which is why interactive borders must use #8B918C, not the 1.19:1 hairline #E2E5E0.

references.name: WCAG 2.2 SC 2.4.13 Focus Appearance + 2.4.11 Focus Not Obscured
references.whyItMatters: Sets the size/contrast bar for the focus ring and forbids removing focus without a compliant replacement; drives the 2px ring + double-ring-on-green spec.

references.name: WCAG 2.2 SC 2.5.5 Target Size (AAA) / 2.5.8 (AA, 24px)
references.whyItMatters: Justifies the 44x44px floor and 8px gap for mono buttons and nav rows aimed at a high-standards security audience.

references.name: WCAG 2.2 SC 1.4.10 Reflow
references.whyItMatters: No loss of content/function at 320px without 2D scroll; underpins the single-column + table-to-card reflow rules at 390px.

references.name: MDN prefers-reduced-motion
references.whyItMatters: Canonical guidance for the global motion reset and the progressive-enhancement reveal pattern that prevents opacity:0 content bleed.

references.name: WebAIM Contrast Checker
references.whyItMatters: Independent verifier for the exact hex/ratio pairs in this spec so the brief's numbers can be reproduced before shipping.

references.name: Vercel Geist Mono and Linear as mono/minimal exemplars
references.whyItMatters: Reference for making an all-monospace, near-monochrome interface read as premium and iconic via spacing, negative tracking, and restraint rather than color or ornament.

pitfalls: Using #11875A for small links, labels or badge text: it is 4.25:1 and silently fails AA on the most-used semantic color. Switch to #0B6B45 for any green text <=18px.
Leaving scroll-reveal sections at opacity:0 by default: when JS fails or prefers-reduced-motion is set, the content disappears (the exact W921 bleed). Default to visible; animate only as enhancement.
Bordering inputs, toggles or segmented controls with only the #E2E5E0 hairline (1.19:1): fails 1.4.11. Interactive borders need #8B918C (3:1).
Stripping focus outlines for looks, or signaling focus with color change alone: breaks keyboard users and 2.4.13. Always render the 2px ring (double-ring on green/dark).
Mixing 4-5 weights or bolding everything: mono goes muddy. Hold to 400/500/600 and create hierarchy with size + space.
Tables that overflow horizontally at 390px with no affordance, or that shrink below 13px to fit: reflow to key:value cards or give an explicit scroll region with edge fade.
Choosing heading levels by visual size (h2 -> h4 skips): corrupts the screen-reader outline. Size via class, keep levels sequential, one h1 per page.
Dumping legal pages as undivided clause walls: reads thin and wordy simultaneously and bounces reviewers. Use the TL;DR + ToC + collapsible shell.
Stacking multiple competing CTAs per screen: dilutes the single 'Book the audit' action. One primary green button per viewport.
Introducing any second hue, gradient or foil: breaks the iconic single-accent system. Green must stay scarce (CTA + pass-state only); everything else is ink on paper.
Inputs below 16px (triggers iOS auto-zoom), targets below 44px, or adjacent targets with <8px gap: degrades mobile trust.
Em-dashes, eyebrow clutter, and the word 'honest/honesty' in copy: violates house style. Use colons/periods, restrained labels, and 'limits/constraints/stated plainly'.
Captioning at #565B57 below 12px or using #8B918C for any real copy: the first loses its 6.5:1 safety margin, the second (3.0:1) is non-text only.
directives: [TOKENS] :root light set -> --paper:#F7F8F6; --paper-raised:#FFFFFF; --paper-sink:#F1F2EF; --ink:#0F1411; --ink-2:#3A413D; --ink-3:#565B57; --ink-faint:#8B918C; --accent:#11875A; --accent-text:#0B6B45; --accent-tint:#E9F3EE; --hairline:#E2E5E0; --border-strong:#8B918C; --on-accent:#FFFFFF.

[A11Y-CONTRAST] Verified ratios on #F7F8F6: #0F1411=17.5:1, #3A413D=9.8:1, #565B57=6.5:1, #8B918C=3.0:1, #11875A=4.25:1, #0B6B45=6.15:1, #FFFFFF-on-#11875A=4.53:1, #E2E5E0=1.19:1. Body/headings #0F1411; secondary paragraphs #3A413D; meta/timestamps/labels >=12px #565B57; disabled/decorative/borders #8B918C only.

[A11Y-GREEN] Green text <=18px must be #0B6B45; #11875A allowed only for fills, icons, UI borders and text >=24px. Verified pill = #0B6B45 glyph+label on #E9F3EE. Pass/fail must also carry a glyph (check / dot), never color alone.

[A11Y-BUTTON] Primary: background #11875A, label #FFFFFF weight 500 >=15px, min-height 44px, padding 12px 20px, radius 8px. Hover darken to #0F7A50; active #0C6A45. Disabled: #EDEFEC bg, #8B918C label, no green. Secondary/ghost: transparent bg, 1px #8B918C border, #0F1411 label.

[A11Y-FOCUS] :focus-visible{outline:2px solid #11875A;outline-offset:2px} On green/dark surfaces use box-shadow:0 0 0 2px #F7F8F6,0 0 0 4px #0F1411;outline:none. Never outline:none without a replacement; suppress mouse :focus, keep :focus-visible.

[A11Y-TARGETS] All buttons/nav/icon-controls >=44x44px (AAA 2.5.5); icon-only 44x44 with 24px glyph; >=8px gap between adjacent targets. Inline prose links exempt but mobile nav rows reach 44px via vertical padding.

[A11Y-MOTION] Global reset: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce){*,*::before,*::after{animation-duration:.001ms!important;animation-iteration-count:1!important;transition-duration:.001ms!important;scroll-behavior:auto!important}}. Reveal classes apply opacity:1 by default and transition only when motion is allowed and JS loaded.

[A11Y-HEADINGS] Exactly one h1 = page promise. Sequential levels, no skips, size via class not tag. Sections=h2, sub-points=h3. Visually-hidden h2 for Proof bar and FAQ. Nav/footer use <nav aria-label>, not headings.

[A11Y-TYPE] Body 16px/1.65, letter-spacing -0.01em, measure max 66ch. Headings weight 600, line-height 1.08-1.12, letter-spacing -0.02em via clamp() (h1 clamp(34px,6vw,60px), h2 clamp(24px,3.5vw,34px), h3 20px). Eyebrow/label 12px, +0.08em, uppercase, #565B57. Numerals/IDs/hashes font-feature-settings:'tnum'. Weights restricted to 400/500/600.

[A11Y-BORDERS] Dividers and card outlines #E2E5E0 (decorative). Inputs/toggles/segmented controls border 1px #8B918C (3:1); focus adds #11875A ring. Cards: #FFFFFF fill, 1px #E2E5E0, shadow 0 1px 2px rgba(15,20,17,.04), radius 12px.

[A11Y-BACKDROP] Flat #F7F8F6, no gradient/foil. Optional hero-only 1px grid at #0F1411 @0.04 or a single hairline rule under the nav. Separate sections with whitespace (s8/s6) + occasional #E2E5E0 hairline, not boxes.

[A11Y-REFLOW-390] <=640px single column; container padding 20px; body >=16px; no horizontal scroll except opt-in tables; grids 1-col <=640, 2-col 641-960, full >=961; grid gap 20px mobile / 28px desktop; card padding 20px.

[A11Y-TABLES] <=640px convert rows to key:value cards (label #565B57 11px uppercase +0.06em, value #0F1411). For true 2D grids use role='region' aria-label tabindex=0 + overflow-x:auto + sticky first col + right-edge fade. Never <13px.

[A11Y-NAV] Desktop inline >=961px (6 items max). <=960px collapse to 44px hamburger -> full-height drawer: 44px stacked rows, focus-trap, Esc closes, body scroll-lock, primary CTA pinned at drawer bottom.

[IA-NAV] Primary nav (<=6): How it works | Pricing | Verify | Security | Docs | [Book the audit]. Footer 4 cols: Product (How it works, Platform, Checks, Report, Pricing) / Trust (Verify, Security, Trust, Status, Transparency log) / Legal (Privacy, Terms, DPA, BAA, SLA, Subprocessors, Acceptable use) / Company (Research, Changelog, Careers, Contact).

[IA-/] Hero(problem h1 + signed-report artifact + 'Book the audit' primary / 'View a sample report' ghost) -> Proof bar(Ed25519 signed | fixed fee | days not weeks) -> Problem(the stalled $100-500k deal) -> How it works(3 steps) -> What we check(6-tile preview -> /checks) -> The report(anatomy block + 'Verify a report' -> /verify) -> For founders / For buyers(2 cards -> /solutions) -> Outcome(1 metric + 1 quote) -> Security & trust strip(-> /security /trust) -> FAQ(5 objections) -> Final CTA. Cap ~9 sections.

[IA-/how-it-works] Intro(1 line + timeline visual) -> Step 1 Scope(inputs, day 0) -> Step 2 Audit(what runs, 2-3 days) -> Step 3 Signed report(deliverable) -> What's inside the report -> Timeline/SLA -> What it is not(scope limits) -> CTA.

[IA-/verify] H1 'Verify a kolm report' -> client-side drop/paste verifier -> result states(valid / signed-by / tampered) -> how verification works(Ed25519, offline, public key) -> public key + fingerprint(kid -> /keys) -> issuer trust chain -> verify via CLI/API snippet -> CTA.

[IA-/security] H1 -> posture summary(at a glance) -> data handling(access, retention, deletion) -> infrastructure(hosting, isolation, encryption in transit/at rest) -> access control(least privilege, key mgmt) -> vulnerability mgmt + disclosure -> subprocessors(-> /subprocessors) -> compliance status(state plainly what is and isn't in place) -> threat model(-> /security/threat-model) -> contact security.

[IA-/security/threat-model] H1 -> scope & assets -> trust boundaries(diagram) -> adversaries & assumptions -> threats table(threat -> mitigation, STRIDE-style) -> key/signing custody(Ed25519) -> out of scope -> residual risk -> report an issue.

[IA-/trust] H1 'Trust center' -> live status(-> /status) -> report verification(-> /verify) -> security overview(-> /security) -> subprocessors -> legal & DPAs(-> /dpa /baa) -> transparency log(-> /transparency-log) -> public key + fingerprint -> contact.

[IA-/enterprise] H1 'Hand security review a report it can verify' -> how review groups use it -> what the report proves(mapped to their checklist) -> verification(offline, no portal login) -> procurement docs(DPA/BAA/SLA links) -> pilot/onboarding -> CTA 'Talk to us'.

[IA-/pricing] H1 + one-line model(single fixed fee) -> the plan(one card: price, included, turnaround SLA) -> what's checked(-> /checks) -> add-ons(re-audit, BAA, SAML) -> compare(DIY vs kolm vs consultancy: time/cost/output) -> billing FAQ -> CTA.

[IA-/report] H1 'The evidence report' -> sample artifact(full) -> anatomy(annotated: header, checks, verdicts, signature block) -> verdict semantics(pass/flag/n-a) -> verification(-> /verify) -> how buyers read it -> download sample(PDF/JSON) -> CTA.

[IA-/checks] H1 'What we check' -> coverage statement -> category groups(Identity & secrets, Data handling, Model/agent safety, Infra, Logging) each a collapsible list of checks(id + one line) -> mapping(SOC2/ISO/OWASP where relevant) -> what we don't check(scope limits) -> CTA.
[IA-/research] H1 -> thesis(why agent security review is broken) -> notes list(date + title + 1 line, grouped into 2-3 pillars to avoid thinness) -> method/benchmark callout -> subscribe -> CTA.

[IA-/contact] H1 -> primary path('Book the audit' -> calendar/form) -> form(name, company, deal size, deadline, message) -> other channels(sales, security@, support) -> response-time expectation -> legal entity/address.

[IA-/platform] H1(what the engine is) -> overview(pipeline diagram scope->run->sign->verify) -> capabilities(checks engine, signing, verifier) -> inputs/integrations -> platform security(-> /security) -> for builders(API/CLI -> /docs) -> CTA.

[IA-/docs] H1 + search -> quickstart(verify a report in 60s) -> concepts(report, verdict, signature, key) -> verify(CLI/API/offline) -> report schema(JSON) -> checks reference(-> /checks) -> keys & rotation -> changelog(-> /changelog) -> support.
[IA-/changelog] H1 -> filter(product/checks/security) -> reverse-chron entries(date, version tag, grouped Added/Changed/Fixed/Security, one line each) -> RSS/subscribe.
[IA-/status] H1 + overall badge(operational) -> component table(API, verifier, signing, site) -> 90-day uptime -> incident history -> subscribe.

[IA-/privacy] Shell: H1 + last-updated + TL;DR box -> sticky ToC -> sections(data collected, use, sharing/subprocessors, retention, rights, transfers, security, children, changes, contact/DPO) -> related-legal footer.
[IA-/terms] Shell -> sections(acceptance, service, fees, IP, warranties/disclaimer, liability cap, indemnity, term/termination, governing law, changes, contact).

[IA-/dpa] Shell + 'Download/sign DPA' CTA -> sections(roles, scope/duration, processing details, subprocessors link, security-measures annex, transfers/SCCs, audit rights, sub-breach, return/deletion).
[IA-/baa] Shell + 'Request BAA' CTA -> sections(HIPAA definitions, permitted uses, safeguards, breach notification, subcontractors, term/termination, return/destruction).
[IA-/sla] Shell -> summary(uptime % + turnaround commitment) -> definitions -> availability target & measurement -> credits table -> support response targets -> exclusions -> claim process.
[IA-/acceptable-use] Shell -> prohibited uses list -> security/abuse -> enforcement -> reporting -> changes.
[IA-/subprocessors] Shell + 'Subscribe to changes' -> table(name, purpose, data, region) -> vetting -> notification policy -> contact.
[IA-/transparency-log] H1 + what this log is -> append-only entries(date, event: key rotation, issuance counts, incidents, gov requests) -> verify log integrity -> subscribe.
[IA-/careers] H1 + 1-line mission -> why kolm(2-3 points) -> open roles(list, or 'no roles open, reach us here') -> how we work -> apply/contact.
[IA-/404] One screen: H1 'Page not found' + 1 line + search -> top destinations(Verify, How it works, Pricing, Docs, Status) -> contact. On-brand, never a dead end.

[IA-/solutions/ai-vendors] H1 'Unblock the deal stuck in security review' -> the stall(founder pain) -> how kolm fixes it -> what you hand the buyer -> timeline/price -> proof/quote -> CTA 'Book the audit'.

[IA-/solutions/enterprise-buyers] H1 'Verify a vendor in minutes, not weeks' -> the review backlog -> how to verify a kolm report -> coverage vs your checklist -> trust/verification -> CTA 'Request a vendor report / Talk to us'.

[IA-GLOBAL] One primary green CTA per viewport; every page ends in a single CTA band. Min section floor = heading + 2-4 sentences or a 3-tile row, else merge. Flagship <=9 sections, sub-pages 5-8, legal via summary+ToC+collapsible shell. No em-dashes, no eyebrow clutter, avoid 'honest/honesty' (use 'limits/constraints/stated plainly').
